top_signals.hypothesis: FCR decreased. Validate unresolved downstream issues, policy/process ambiguity, agent knowledge gaps, and complex contact mix.
top_signals.why_this_matters: A delivery-related FCR drop can create repeat contacts and weaken trust because the underlying order issue may still be unresolved.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Check delayed-order status, ETA accuracy, courier/warehouse incidents, repeat-contact samples, and whether customers needed follow-up after the first response.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Ask CS Operations and Fulfillment Ops to review late-delivery repeat contacts and identify the top unresolved driver.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Review movement alongside sample size, adjacent weeks, and operational context.

top_signals.hypothesis: Compensation exposure increased. Check whether this is driven by higher contact volume, higher compensation per contact, policy change, or late delivery/cancellation mix.
top_signals.why_this_matters: Delivery compensation cost can signal expensive service recovery caused by operational failures.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Decompose cost into contact volume and compensation per contact; review delivery/cancellation policy usage and large goodwill outliers.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Split the increase into volume versus compensation-per-contact, then review high-cost policy or goodwill cases with Finance/Policy.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Volume effect: contacts -12; compensation/contact 5.3 vs 3.7 baseline.

top_signals.hypothesis: Possible delivery reliability pressure. Validate late-order rate, courier incidents, and warehouse capacity.
top_signals.why_this_matters: A higher contact rate means support demand is rising relative to orders.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Compare contact movement with order movement; check product/process friction and reason mix shift.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Validate order denominator and identify which contact reasons are creating demand above order growth.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Contacts changed +9; orders changed +64. Interpret relative demand, not contacts alone.

top_signals.hypothesis: AHT increased. Validate contact complexity, new-agent share, backlog pressure, process/tool issues, and contact mix.
top_signals.why_this_matters: Delivery contacts with longer AHT can consume capacity quickly because agents often need partner checks and customer follow-up.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Check whether agents are spending more time tracing delivery status, escalating to partners, or handling unclear ETA information.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Review ticket samples by reason and compare staffing, tool incidents, and escalation mix before proposing a process action.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Compare AHT movement with contact volume change (+9) and reason mix before concluding process impact.

top_signals.hypothesis: Cancellation rate increased in Netherlands. Validate fulfillment, partner, delivery, and policy/process changes at country-week grain.
top_signals.why_this_matters: Cancellation rate is an order-level business outcome and should be investigated at country-week grain.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Validate at country-week grain; check fulfillment, partner, delivery, and policy/process changes.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Validate the country-week order grain and review fulfillment or policy changes before escalation.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Country-week order metric repeated across reasons; validate using country-week totals, not summed reason rows.

top_signals.hypothesis: Backlog pressure increased. Validate staffing coverage, unresolved queue age, and whether AHT or volume also rose.
top_signals.why_this_matters: Backlog creates delayed resolution and future customer experience risk.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Check staffing coverage, queue age, contact volume, and AHT movement.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Monitor and compare with adjacent weeks.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Review movement alongside sample size, adjacent weeks, and operational context.

top_signals.hypothesis: CSAT decreased. Validate survey count, service quality themes, and specific reason/country friction.
top_signals.why_this_matters: CSAT movement affects customer trust, but response count determines reliability.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Check survey response count, complaint themes, country/reason friction, and whether FCR or AHT also deteriorated.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Check response count and sample recent low-score contacts before linking the movement to service quality.
top_signals.decomposition_note: CSAT response count: 12 latest vs 6.8 baseline.

top_signals.hypothesis: Possible cancellation intent increase. Validate cancellation policy, supply issues, and order mix.
top_signals.why_this_matters: A higher contact rate means support demand is rising relative to orders.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Compare contact movement with order movement; check product/process friction and reason mix shift.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Validate order denominator and identify which contact reasons are creating demand above order growth.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Contacts changed +0; orders changed -5. Interpret relative demand, not contacts alone.

top_signals.hypothesis: FCR decreased. Validate unresolved downstream issues, policy/process ambiguity, agent knowledge gaps, and complex contact mix.
top_signals.why_this_matters: A delivery-related FCR drop can create repeat contacts and weaken trust because the underlying order issue may still be unresolved.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Check delayed-order status, ETA accuracy, courier/warehouse incidents, repeat-contact samples, and whether customers needed follow-up after the first response.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Ask CS Operations and Fulfillment Ops to review late-delivery repeat contacts and identify the top unresolved driver.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Review movement alongside sample size, adjacent weeks, and operational context.

top_signals.hypothesis: Compensation exposure increased. Check whether this is driven by higher contact volume, higher compensation per contact, policy change, or late delivery/cancellation mix.
top_signals.why_this_matters: Delivery compensation cost can signal expensive service recovery caused by operational failures.
top_signals.suggested_validation: Decompose cost into contact volume and compensation per contact; review delivery/cancellation policy usage and large goodwill outliers.
top_signals.recommended_next_action: Split the increase into volume versus compensation-per-contact, then review high-cost policy or goodwill cases with Finance/Policy.
top_signals.decomposition_note: Volume effect: contacts +9; compensation/contact 4.1 vs 5.0 baseline.

pattern_insights.summary: Late delivery FCR deteriorated across 4 countries.
pattern_insights.recommended_review: Check delayed-order status, ETA accuracy, courier/warehouse incidents, repeat-contact samples, and whether customers needed follow-up after the first response.

pattern_insights.summary: Refund request FCR deteriorated across 4 countries.
pattern_insights.recommended_review: Review reopened cases, downstream unresolved issues, policy/process ambiguity, agent knowledge gaps, and complex contact mix.
Review cancellation flow ownership, refund routing, macro accuracy, reopened cases, and unresolved follow-up reasons.

pattern_insights.summary: Late delivery Compensation cost deteriorated across 2 countries.
pattern_insights.recommended_review: Decompose cost into contact volume and compensation per contact; review delivery/cancellation policy usage and large goodwill outliers.

low_confidence_signals.hypothesis: CSAT moved on a low survey count. Treat as a validation prompt, not a performance conclusion.
low_confidence_signals.why_this_matters: CSAT movement affects customer trust, but response count determines reliability.
low_confidence_signals.suggested_validation: Low-confidence signal: validate sample size first. Check survey response count, complaint themes, country/reason friction, and whether FCR or AHT also deteriorated.
low_confidence_signals.recommended_next_action: Confirm sample size and source completeness before using this in the weekly business narrative.
low_confidence_signals.decomposition_note: CSAT response count: 4 latest vs 4.8 baseline.

method: Latest complete week compared with the average of the previous four weeks at country and contact-reason grain.
